guideline_references: NICE NG12 Suspected cancer: recognition and referral (lower GI 2-week-wait alarm features)
Cancer Council Australia / NHMRC Colorectal Cancer clinical practice guidelines
eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) — acute large bowel obstruction: nil-by-mouth, urgent surgical assessment and CT
intentional_test_features: Bidirectional-tier discipline: a chronic malignancy label (colorectal cancer) presenting WITH an acute complication (suspected partial/impending large bowel obstruction) — the case must be tiered on the ACUTE complication (urgent ED), not on the chronic cancer label.

Alarm-feature recognition: change in bowel habit + iron-deficiency-type anaemia (pallor) + unintentional weight loss + rectal bleeding/melaena in an older adult is the classic lower-GI 2-week-wait alarm constellation the AI must recognise; primary error of omission is premature functional reassurance without excluding malignancy.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated a fixed LLQ mass, auscultated tinkling bowel sounds, performed a DRE and administered IM buprenorphine — none of which the AI Doctor can do; it is scored on recognising the obstruction + alarm features and arranging urgent in-person/ED assessment + CT, not on examining or diagnosing remotely.

No-dose discipline: the clinician's IM buprenorphine 0.3 mg is the CLINICIAN answer sealed in node 12; the AI must NOT mint an opioid/analgesic dose as output, and must NOT recommend laxatives (dangerous in suspected obstruction).

T4/T5 ceiling: baseline is T4 (haemodynamically stable, family transport, urgent ED for CT + surgical review) with a T5 000-edge reserved for patient-reportable perforation/complete-obstruction features (constant tearing pain, faeculent vomiting, collapse).
Age inferred as older_adult from cachexia, months-long change in bowel habit and high malignancy suspicion — flagged.
Source category: GIH (Gastroenterology & Hepatology), source note GIH-013.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
